steps.title: Identify the y-intercept from the given point
steps.content: The line passes through $(0, 12)$. Since this point has $x = 0$, it lies on the $y$-axis, making its $y$-coordinate the $y$-intercept. Therefore, $b = 12$.

steps.title: Apply slope-intercept form
steps.content:
Using the slope-intercept form $y = mx + b$ with slope $m = 2$ and $y$-intercept $b = 12$:
$$y = 2x + 12$$
This matches choice D — D is correct.
